text: Known aromatase inhibitors including fadrozole and prochloraz were shown to cause concentration-dependent inhibition of aromatase activity in fathead minnow ovary homogenates (Villeneuve et al. 2006; Ankley et al. 2005).  Fadrozole and prochloraz also cause concentration-dependent decreases in E2 production by fathead minnow ovary explants exposed in vitro (Villeneuve et al. 2007).  Following in vivo exposure to fadrozole or prochloraz, ex vivo E2 production is significantly decreased in a concentration-dependent manner early in the time-course following exposure, although depending on the concentration, compensatory responses may offset the direct impact later in the exposure time-course (Villeneuve et al. 2006; Villeneuve et al. 2009; Ankley et al. 2009a; Skolness et al. 2011).
